stemHtml: A Mode S transponder knows that it is receiving interrogations from a Mode S ground interrogator because:

options.a: The ground interrogator will transmit extra pulses once it has had the initial reply from the airborne transponder.
options.b: The ground interrogator transmits the same pulses for all modes, but for Mode S, the pulses are stronger.
options.c: Extra pulses are transmitted directly following the Mode A and Mode C pulses.
options.d: Only pulses specific to Mode S are transmitted by the ground interrogator.
